water.name: Eel River
aliases: North Fork Middle Fork Eel River

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Eel River above Lake Pillsbury
reaches.fromDesc: Eel River above Lake Pillsbury and tributaries to Lake Pillsbury (Lake Co.)
reaches.toDesc: Headwaters of the Eel River and its tributaries feeding Lake Pillsbury

reaches.name: Van Duzen River (upper, above Eaton Falls)
reaches.fromDesc: Van Duzen River main stem and tributaries upstream of Eaton Falls (about 1/2 mile upstream of the mouth of the South Fork / Little Van Duzen River)
reaches.toDesc: Upstream to about 1/2 mile downstream of Dinsmore (Humboldt and Trinity Cos.)

reaches.name: Eel River Middle Fork and specified tributaries (upper, headwaters)
reaches.fromDesc: Middle Fork Eel River tributaries (Hammerhorn Creek, Rattlesnake Creek, Beaver Creek, Fly Creek, Bar Creek) upstream of the USFS M1 Road crossing; Middle Fork main stem and tributaries upstream of the mouth of Uhl Creek; Balm of Gilead Creek upstream of the falls 1.2 miles from its mouth; North Fork of the Middle Fork upstream of the mouth of Willow Creek (Mendocino and Trinity Cos.)
reaches.toDesc: Headwaters of the Middle Fork Eel River system in the Yolla Bolly-Middle Eel Wilderness

reaches.name: Eel River main stem, mouth to Fulmor Road
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth of the Eel River (Pacific Ocean, Humboldt Co.)
reaches.toDesc: Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River

reaches.name: Eel River main stem, Fulmor Road to South Fork Eel River
reaches.fromDesc: Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River
reaches.toDesc: Confluence with the South Fork Eel River near Dyerville (Humboldt Co.)

reaches.name: Eel River main stem, South Fork Eel River to Cape Horn Dam
reaches.fromDesc: Confluence with the South Fork Eel River near Dyerville
reaches.toDesc: Cape Horn Dam / Van Arsdale Reservoir near Potter Valley (Mendocino Co.)

reaches.name: Van Duzen River (lower, junction with Eel River to Bridgeville)
reaches.fromDesc: Junction of the Van Duzen River with the Eel River
reaches.toDesc: End of Golden Gate Drive near Bridgeville, approximately 4,000 feet upstream of the Little Golden Gate Bridge (Humboldt Co.)

reaches.name: South Fork Eel River, mouth to Rattlesnake Creek
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth of the South Fork Eel River (confluence with the main stem Eel River near Dyerville)

reaches.name: Middle Fork Eel River main stem, mouth to Bar Creek
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth of the Middle Fork Eel River (confluence with the main stem Eel River near Dos Rios)

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(b)(45) — Eel River (Humboldt, Lake, Mendocino and Trinity Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (45) Eel River (Humboldt, Lake, Mendocino and Trinity Cos.). (A) Eel River above Lake Pillsbury and tributaries to Lake Pillsbury (Lake Co.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout (B) Van Duzen River. Main stem and tributaries upstream of Eaton Falls, located about 1/2 mile upstream of the mouth of the South Fork (Little Van Duzen) and 1/2 miles downstream of Dinsmore (Humboldt and Trinity Cos). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout (C) Eel River Middle Fork.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(40) — Eel River (Humboldt, Lake, Mendocino and Trinity Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (40) Eel River (Humboldt, Lake, Mendocino and Trinity Cos.). Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions also apply. All waters of the Eel River Drainage except those listed below are closed to all fishing. (A) Main stem. 1. From mouth to Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through the Fri. preceding the fourth Sat. in May. Only barbless hooks may be used from fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. 2. From Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River, to South Fork Eel River. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. 3. From South Fork Eel River to Cape Horn Dam. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31 and fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. Apr. 1 through the fourth Fri. in May and Oct. 1 through Dec. 31. Closed to all fishing. (B) Van Duzen River. 1. Main stem from its junction with the Eel River to the end of Golden Gate Drive near Bridgeville (approximately 4,000 feet upstream from the Little Golden Gate Bridge). Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (C) South Fork Eel River from mouth to Rattlesnake Creek. Also see Low-Flow Restrictions, Section 8.00(a). Apr. 1 to fourth Fri. in May. Closed to all fishing. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (D) Middle Fork Eel River. 1. Middle Fork main stem from mouth to Bar Creek. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Jan. 1 through May 31 and Jul. 16 through Sep. 30. At all times, only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. Jun. 1 through Jul. 15 and Oct. through Dec. 31. Closed to all fishing.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00(a) — Eel River, Mad River, Mattole River, Redwood Creek, Smith River and Van Duzen River: Special Low Flow Conditions Closures
sources.quotedText: (a) Eel River, Mad River, Mattole River, Redwood Creek, Smith River and Van Duzen River. Stream closures: Special Low Flow Conditions.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
Friends of the Eel River — The Eel River
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 251974, "Eel River", Stream, Humboldt/Mendocino/Lake/Trinity Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":251974,"gaz_name":"Eel River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Humboldt"} — geometry polyline; mouth-end point (Web Mercator -13838381.4529, 4959609.0959) converts to lon -124.3123, lat 40.6415, at the river's mouth on the Pacific Ocean near Table Bluff, replacing the file's previous coordinate (-123.2100, 39.8600), which was off by ~128 km (it fell near the Middle Fork headwaters, not the main-stem mouth).

groups.humanSummary: Eel River above Lake Pillsbury and its tributaries to the lake (Lake Co.): open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February, artificial lures only, 2-trout limit.
groups.verbatimText: (A) Eel River above Lake Pillsbury and tributaries to Lake Pillsbury (Lake Co.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

groups.humanSummary: Van Duzen River main stem and tributaries upstream of Eaton Falls (Humboldt and Trinity Cos.): open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30, artificial lures only, 2-trout limit.
groups.verbatimText: (B) Van Duzen River. Main stem and tributaries upstream of Eaton Falls, located about 1/2 mile upstream of the mouth of the South Fork (Little Van Duzen) and 1/2 miles downstream of Dinsmore (Humboldt and Trinity Cos). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

groups.humanSummary: Eel River Middle Fork and four specified tributary segments (Mendocino and Trinity Cos.): open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February, artificial lures only, 2-trout limit.
groups.verbatimText: (C) Eel River Middle Fork. 1. Eel River Middle Fork tributaries (Hammerhorn Creek, Rattlesnake Creek, Beaver Creek, Fly Creek, and Bar Creek) upstream of USFS M1 Road crossing (Mendocino and Trinity Cos). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout 2. Eel River Middle Fork and tributaries upstream of mouth of Uhl Creek (Trinity Co.) Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout 3. Balm of Gilead Creek, upstream of falls located 1.2 miles from mouth and one mile downstream of Wright's Valley Trail crossing (Trinity Co.) Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout 4. Eel River North Fork of the Middle Fork upstream of mouth of Willow Creek (Trinity Co.) Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

groups.humanSummary: Eel River main stem from mouth to Fulmor Road: open all year; artificial lures with barbless hooks only Apr. 1 through the Friday preceding the fourth Saturday in May, barbless hooks only the rest of the year; catch-and-release Chinook Salmon; 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(a) low-flow closures.
groups.verbatimText: 1. From mouth to Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through the Fri. preceding the fourth Sat. in May. Only barbless hooks may be used from fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Eel River main stem from Fulmor Road to South Fork Eel River: open all year; artificial lures with barbless hooks only Apr. 1 through Sep. 30, barbless hooks only Oct. 1 through Mar. 31; catch-and-release Chinook Salmon; 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(a) low-flow closures.
groups.verbatimText: 2. From Fulmor Road, at its paved junction with the south bank of the Eel River, to South Fork Eel River. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Eel River main stem from South Fork Eel River to Cape Horn Dam: open Jan. 1 through Mar. 31 and fourth Saturday in May through Sep. 30 (artificial lures with barbless hooks only); closed to all fishing Apr. 1 through the fourth Friday in May and Oct. 1 through Dec. 31; catch-and-release Chinook Salmon and 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession) during open periods.
groups.verbatimText: 3. From South Fork Eel River to Cape Horn Dam. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31 and fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. Apr. 1 through the fourth Fri. in May and Oct. 1 through Dec. 31. Closed to all fishing.

groups.humanSummary: Van Duzen River main stem from its junction with the Eel River to the end of Golden Gate Drive near Bridgeville: open fourth Saturday in May through Mar. 31; artificial lures with barbless hooks only through Sep. 30, barbless hooks only thereafter; catch-and-release Chinook Salmon; 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(a) low-flow closures.
groups.verbatimText: 1. Main stem from its junction with the Eel River to the end of Golden Gate Drive near Bridgeville (approximately 4,000 feet upstream from the Little Golden Gate Bridge). Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: South Fork Eel River from mouth to Rattlesnake Creek: closed to all fishing Apr. 1 to the fourth Friday in May; open fourth Saturday in May through Mar. 31 with artificial lures with barbless hooks only through Sep. 30, barbless hooks only thereafter; catch-and-release Chinook Salmon; 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(a) low-flow closures.
groups.verbatimText: (C) South Fork Eel River from mouth to Rattlesnake Creek. Also see Low-Flow Restrictions, Section 8.00(a). Apr. 1 to fourth Fri. in May. Closed to all fishing. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Sep. 30. Only barbless hooks may be used from Oct. 1 through Mar. 31. Catch and release of Chinook Salmon. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Middle Fork Eel River main stem from mouth to Bar Creek: open Jan. 1 through May 31 and Jul. 16 through Sep. 30, only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used at all times; closed to all fishing Jun. 1 through Jul. 15 and Oct. through Dec. 31; 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(a) low-flow closures.
groups.verbatimText: 1. Middle Fork main stem from mouth to Bar Creek. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Jan. 1 through May 31 and Jul. 16 through Sep. 30. At all times, only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. Jun. 1 through Jul. 15 and Oct. through Dec. 31. Closed to all fishing.

parameters.note: All waters of the Eel River Drainage other than the main stem, Van Duzen River, South Fork Eel River, and Middle Fork Eel River segments specifically listed under §7.40(b)(40) (and the upper trout segments listed under §7.50(b)(45)) are closed to all fishing.

regulations.humanSummary: All waters of the Eel River Drainage except the specifically listed segments are closed to all fishing.
regulations.verbatimText: All waters of the Eel River Drainage except those listed below are closed to all fishing.

parameters.description: The main stem, Van Duzen River, South Fork Eel River, and Middle Fork Eel River segments listed under §7.40(b)(40) are also subject to 14 CCR §8.00(a), the Eel/Mad/Mattole/Redwood Creek/Smith/Van Duzen special low-flow closure provision, under which fishing may be closed when streamflow at a designated gauge falls below a prescribed threshold.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(a) low-flow stream closures.
regulations.verbatimText: Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions also apply.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained under the hatchery trout/steelhead limits described in this file; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
